name: David Richardson
backstory: Works as a nurse at a clinic in Webster Groves where he's been for six years. Spends most weekends volunteering with the plant care team at the Missouri Botanical Garden, finding peace in the greenhouse work.
sampleText: Heading to the garden this Saturday. You free?

name: Jennifer Mitchell
backstory: Moved to Maplewood ten years ago for an insurance firm job downtown and never left. She's become a fixture at the Pageant, catching most major shows and knowing half the bar staff by name.
sampleText: The Pageant has an amazing lineup next month. Want to grab drinks before?

name: Kevin Chen
backstory: Works in logistics and transport for a regional company based in St. Charles, managing shipments across the Midwest. His weekends are for cycling the Katy Trail with his camera, capturing river views and testing new photography techniques.
sampleText: Just did a full Katy Trail loop this morning, the light was perfect. Got some incredible shots at the overlook, including one with the river mist that I'm really happy with.

name: Marcus Thompson
backstory: Been in office admin roles around the St. Louis area for over a decade, currently based in Florissant. His early morning runs through Forest Park before work have become his anchor routine, and he's known locally for his smooth 90s R&B renditions at karaoke nights.
sampleText: yo so like i've been trying to get my 5k time down and it's finally happening, did like 19:45 this morning which is crazy because last month i was hitting like 21 minutes consistently, been hitting forest park at like 6am before work and it's actually making a difference you know? anyway we doing karaoke thursday or what

name: Aliyah Jackson
backstory: Started in retail management in South City and quickly moved up due to her strong leadership and organizational skills. Recently got into pickleball with coworkers and now runs Forest Park trails on weekends to balance desk work with actual movement.
sampleText: getting a crew together for pickleball next saturday at the south city courts if you down. we've been running it every other weekend and it's honestly so much fun
